things.name: Great Reformed Church
things.description: Hungary's largest Protestant church (1821) and national symbol of Hungarian Calvinism; it was here in 1849 that Kossuth declared Hungarian independence

things.name: Reformed College (Debrecen Calvinist College)
things.description: Historic Protestant college founded in 1538 with a world-class library of over 600,000 volumes and a museum of the Reformation's role in Hungarian history

things.name: Debrecen Zoo and Amusement Park
things.description: One of Hungary's finest zoos with diverse animal collections and an adjacent amusement park in the Great Forest park north of the city centre

things.name: Nagyerdo (Great Forest) Park
things.description: Vast urban forest park with an open-air bath complex, the famous Aquaticum spa, deer enclosures and 19km of walking and cycling paths through mature woodland

things.name: Deri Museum
things.description: Hungary's largest provincial museum with outstanding collections of Hungarian painting, archaeology, ethnography and the famous Munkacsy Christ Trilogy

things.name: Flower Carnival (Viragkarneval)
things.description: Hungary's most spectacular summer festival held on the 20th August national holiday; elaborate flower-decorated floats process through the city before the Great Church

things.name: Piac Street Pedestrian Zone
things.description: Debrecen's main pedestrian street linking the train station to the Great Church; lined with shops, cafes and the city's most elegant 19th and 20th-century facades

things.name: Aquaticum Mediterraneum Spa
things.description: Hungarian award-winning indoor tropical spa with thermal pools, water slides and wellness facilities in a Mediterranean-themed complex in the Great Forest

things.name: Debrecen University Campus
things.description: One of Hungary's oldest and most prestigious universities in an extensive campus north of the centre; the main building is one of Hungary's finest neoclassical structures

things.name: Nagyerdei Stadion
things.description: Home of Debreceni VSC with 20,340 capacity; Hungary's most successful club in the 2000s, competing in the Champions League group stage and Europa League
